milestone_progress: 4/5 phases complete (6, 7, 8, 9 done; 10 fully planned, ready to execute)

completed_tasks.name: Phase 10 smart discuss → CONTEXT.md (16 decisions, 4 grey areas)
UI-SPEC.md drafted, revised, approved 6/6 by ui-checker
RESEARCH.md (HIGH confidence, 9 pitfalls, 17 test cases, validation architecture)
VALIDATION.md derived from RESEARCH §Validation Architecture (Nyquist contract, 17 cases mapped to DBUI-01..05)
5 PLAN.md files in 4 waves via gsd-planner (10-01..10-05)
gsd-plan-checker → VERIFICATION PASSED (15 phase-specific anti-pattern checks + 10 standard dimensions, no revision needed)

remaining_tasks.name: Execute Wave 1: 10-01 (vendor htmx/d3/dagre-d3 + rust-embed dep + VENDOR.md) || 10-02 (ThroughputTracker EWMA + cascade dedup) — parallel via worktree isolation. 10-01 Task 3 is checkpoint:human-verify (browser smoke test for d3 v7 + dagre-d3 0.6.4).
Execute Wave 3: 10-04 (index.html, app.css, app.js, icons.svg, favicon.svg) — Task 4 is checkpoint:human-verify (rendered UI smoke test including XSS test)
Execute Wave 4: 10-05 (tests/test_debug_ui.rs — 15 raw-TCP integration tests + fix stale tests/test_server.rs AppState literal)
gsd-verifier creates VERIFICATION.md, route on status (passed/human_needed/gaps_found)

human_actions_pending.action: Browser smoke test for d3 v7 + dagre-d3 0.6.4 compatibility
human_actions_pending.context: Plan 10-01 Task 3 is checkpoint:human-verify before committing vendored bytes. RESEARCH §A3 flags this as the only HIGH-risk assumption — render a 3-node DAG locally; if it breaks, fall back to d3 v5.

human_actions_pending.action: Browser smoke test for rendered Phase 10 frontend
human_actions_pending.context: Plan 10-04 Task 4 is the second checkpoint:human-verify. Load http://localhost:6401/, verify four tabs render, DAG appears with blue/purple/gray nodes, throughput updates live, pause button works, no console errors. Includes paste-XSS test with <script>alert(1)</script> as entity key.

decisions.decision: Plan structure: 5 plans in 4 waves with 10-01 + 10-02 in parallel Wave 1
decisions.rationale: 10-01 (vendor JS + rust-embed) and 10-02 (ThroughputTracker module) have zero files_modified overlap. 10-03 depends on both. 10-04 depends on 10-03 (consumes its endpoints). 10-05 depends on everything for end-to-end integration tests.

decisions.decision: Pause before executor spawn at 91% context
decisions.rationale: Wave 1 spawns two parallel executors with worktree isolation. Running them with degraded context risks the orchestrator missing wave-completion signals or mishandling the 10-01 checkpoint pause. Phase 10 is the last v1.1 phase — fresh context needed for clean handoff into milestone lifecycle.

decisions.decision: All Phase 10 anti-pattern guardrails encoded into PLAN.md acceptance criteria
decisions.rationale: Plan-checker confirmed: no 16px type token, no 12px spacing token, throughput cascade dedup unit test, list_views() iterated in topology, /debug/memory extended additively, axum 0.8 {*file} syntax, debug-embed feature flag enabled, raw TCP tests, .textContent for XSS safety. Each rule is grep-verifiable in plan acceptance criteria.

next_action: Resume with /gsd-autonomous --from 10 in a fresh session. The autonomous workflow will detect existing CONTEXT.md + UI-SPEC.md + RESEARCH.md + VALIDATION.md + 5 PLAN.md files and pick up at execute-phase Wave 1. After Phase 10 verification passes, the workflow chains into milestone audit → complete → cleanup automatically.
context_notes: Phase 10 is the LAST phase of v1.1 (Composable Pipeline & Event Log). All planning is verified clean — execution is the ONLY remaining work for the entire milestone. Plan-checker accepted the plans on first pass with no revisions, validating the planner's output across 15 phase-specific anti-pattern checks and 10 standard dimensions. Wave structure is dependency-correct: Wave 1 has files_modified zero-overlap, Waves 2/3/4 each strictly depend on prior waves' artifacts. Two checkpoints will pause execution (10-01 Task 3 and 10-04 Task 4) — both browser smoke tests. Ship-ready summary on the other side.
